introSection: Whizzer is a steel roller coaster located at Six Flags Great America in Gurnee, Illinois, USA. It is the only remaining Speed Racer coaster from Schwarzkopf.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Willard's Whizzer opened on May 29, 1976 as one of the park's several opening day attractions. It was named after park owner Willard Marriott.

In 2002, Six Flags announced that Whizzer would be removed to make way for a new attraction (which turned out to be Superman Ultimate Flight). It was scheduled to close on August 11. Following public outcry, the park kept Whizzer open. On September 24, 2002, it announced that it would dismantle ShockWave instead.

The ride's cars and the station were wrapped in 2010 with advertisements for the film "The Other Guys".

American Coaster Enthusiasts awarded the ride ACE Landmark status on August 10, 2012.

At No Coaster Con 2024, Six Flags announced that the classic coaster would get a new control system.

On January 17, 2026 at the No Coaster Con event held by American Coaster Enthusiasts in Rosemont, Illinois, USA, the park announced that the ride would be refurbished with 14 new track pieces.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Black track and supports. The ride used to feature black track and white supports.

fullText:
Whizzer is a steel roller coaster located at Six Flags Great America in Gurnee, Illinois, USA. It is the only remaining Speed Racer coaster from Schwarzkopf.

Willard's Whizzer opened on May 29, 1976 as one of the park's several opening day attractions. It was named after park owner Willard Marriott.

In 2002, Six Flags announced that Whizzer would be removed to make way for a new attraction (which turned out to be Superman Ultimate Flight). It was scheduled to close on August 11. Following public outcry, the park kept Whizzer open. On September 24, 2002, it announced that it would dismantle ShockWave instead.

The ride's cars and the station were wrapped in 2010 with advertisements for the film "The Other Guys".

American Coaster Enthusiasts awarded the ride ACE Landmark status on August 10, 2012.

At No Coaster Con 2024, Six Flags announced that the classic coaster would get a new control system.

On January 17, 2026 at the No Coaster Con event held by American Coaster Enthusiasts in Rosemont, Illinois, USA, the park announced that the ride would be refurbished with 14 new track pieces.



Black track and supports. The ride used to feature black track and white supports.
